session.assistant:
Plan execution complete. Here's a summary of the changes made:
**Problem:** `erk land --up` generated `land.sh` with a hardcoded `cd` path to the child branch's worktree, which broke when (1) branch names contain `/` (creating nested paths), or (2) the worktree didn't exist yet at validation time.
**Fix:** Deferred upstack path resolution to execute time using shell command substitution.
**Changes in `src/erk/cli/commands/land_cmd.py`:**
1. **`_navigate_after_land`** (`land_cmd.py:1226`): When `skip_activation_output=True` (execute mode) with `--up`, now resolves/creates the child worktree, shows the restack tip on stderr, and outputs the path to stdout. The shell script captures this via `TARGET_DIR=$(...)`.
2. **`render_land_execution_script`** (`land_cmd.py:1306`): Added `upstack_navigation: bool` parameter and `target_path: Path | None`. When `upstack_navigation=True`, generates `TARGET_DIR=$(erk exec land-execute ...) || return 1` + `cd "$TARGET_DIR"` instead of `erk exec land-execute ... || return 1` + `cd /hardcoded/path`.
3. **`_land_target`** (`land_cmd.py:1064`): When `target.target_child_branch is not None`, sets `target_path = None` and `upstack_navigation = True`. Removed the buggy fallback `worktrees_dir / branch_name`. Updated dry-run message to `"Would navigate upstack to child branch '{name}'"`.
**Changes in tests:** - Updated all 10 existing `test_render_land_script.py` calls to pass `upstack_navigation=False` - Added 2 new tests: upstack command substitution and non-upstack hardcoded cd - Added 1 new test in `test_cleanup_and_navigate.py` for execute mode with `--up` that verifies the worktree path is output to stdout
PR: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9233
